title: Clinopodium alternipilosum
intro: Clinopodium alternipilosum, also known as variegated calamint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) characterized by its fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Clinopodium alternipilosum include essential oils, particularly borneol and camphor, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, headaches, and skin conditions.
